splits.title: No Enemies
splits.author: Selected
splits.start_boundary: NO ENEMIES. "You have no enemies; alas, my friend,
splits.end_boundary: You've been a coward in the fight. You have no enemies." â€” Selected.

files.notes: This file contains the continuation of 'A Prince of Ur' serialized story by Homespun, followed by a short poem 'No Enemies'. The story appears to be set in ancient Mesopotamia featuring characters Abram and Mardan discussing religious and moral issues. The poem is a brief reflection on having enemies as a sign of having done meaningful work.

splits.title: Guide Lessons - August 1915
splits.start_boundary: Guide Lessons. LESSON I. Work and Business.
splits.end_boundary: land which the Lord thy God giveth to thee.

splits.title: Guide Lessons - September 1915
splits.start_boundary: September Guide Lessons. LESSON I. Work and Business
splits.end_boundary: are you making the most of it?

files.notes: This file contains two separate monthly Guide Lessons sections - August and September 1915. The August lesson focuses on hygiene for women past fifty, while September covers genealogy (heirship rules) and home ethics/art. There's a clear note in the text explaining that both months are published together so readers have lessons one month in advance.
